mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior month signals easier discounting and higher equity present values, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is easing. This fails if rate cuts occur during acute recession scares that overwhelm the valuation effect.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of months with a 3-month FEDFUNDS decline, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL acceleration above recent inflation trend raises real-rate pressure and reduces equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when FEDFUNDS is already elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the sign flips positive when CPIAUCSL inflation rises above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE together with slowing PAYEMS indicates weakening labor income and household demand, which should depress corporate revenues and S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal should be clearer when both labor measures deteriorate at the same time than when only one does.
failure_condition: Reject if joint deterioration in UNRATE and PAYEMS does not improve negative-return hit rate versus either series alone, or if 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of joint-signal cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in RSAFS implies stronger current consumer spending and revenue realization, which should support near-term equity returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails if sales gains come from one-off price increases rather than real demand.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises more than 0.5% month over month
failure_condition: Reject if months with RSAFS growth above 0.5% do not show higher-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal turns negative during repeated sales expansions.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO paired with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates real output growth without overheating, which should lift earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism breaks if industrial output rises only because of inventory restocking while inflation rises too.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if rising INDPRO combined with rising CPIAUCSL loses explanatory power.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and a broader slowdown in cyclically sensitive spending, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days. This fails when housing weakness is offset by strong labor income or policy easing.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below its 6-month average does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the average forward return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a low and flat UNRATE with firm PAYEMS supports resilient consumption and reduces recession risk, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails if labor strength is already fully priced or if rate hikes dominate equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases over the last 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if, during tightening periods, low UNRATE and rising PAYEMS do not improve 5-day SPX returns relative to tightening periods without labor strength, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in INDPRO after a prior slowdown indicates improving real activity and capacity utilization, which should translate into higher equity returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails when production improves only after a prolonged policy shock that keeps valuations compressed.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its level 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO above its 3-month lag does not correspond to positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the rolling 3-year average return is not above zero.
